Name: Jirachi
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Jirachi from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip 3 coins. For each heads, search your discard pile for a [P] Energy card and attach it to Jirachi.
Effects: Choose a number of your opponent’s Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Jirachi. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of those Pokémon and put those cards back into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Magmortar.
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Metagross
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have any [P] Energy attached to your Active Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for that Pokémon is 0.
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Mismagius
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [P] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Mismagius is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Octillery
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Octillery with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Politoed
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose a [W] Pokémon on your Bench and switch it with your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Politoed is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Whenever three or more of these get together, they sing in a loud voice that sounds like bellowing.

Name: Shaymin
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Shaymin from your hand onto your Bench, you may move as many Energy cards attached to your Pokémon as you like to any of your other Pokémon.
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of [F] Energy attached to Sudowoodo.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It disguises itself as a tree to avoid attack. It hates water, so it will disappear if it starts raining.

Name: Torterra
Effects: Remove from Torterra the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

Name: Xatu
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: In South America, it is said that its right eye sees the future and its left eye views the past.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is also Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It can take down any opponent with its powerful poison stingers. It sometimes attacks in swarms.

Name: Blastoise
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [W] Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Blastoise is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Return 2 [W] Energy attached to Blastoise to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It deliberately makes itself heavy so it can withstand the recoil of the water jets it fires.

Name: Crobat
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The development of wings on its legs enables it to fly fast but also makes it tough to stop and rest.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It shoots itself suddenly high into the sky, then plummets down in one fell swoop to strike its prey.

Name: Floatzel
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to Floatzel. This power can’t be used if Floatzel is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may do 50 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, return an Energy card attached to Kingdra to your hand.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps deep on the ocean floor to build its energy. It is said to cause tornadoes as it wakes.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Move an Energy card attached to Lanturn to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea’s surface from a depth of over three miles.

Name: Lucario
Effects: During your next turn, each of Lucario’s attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras, even foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 3 [R] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Although an energetic, skilled swimmer that uses all of its muscles, it lives on dry land.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If approached while asleep, it may awaken and angrily give chase in a groggy state of semi-sleep.

Name: Roserade
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: When you attach a [G] Energy card or [P] Energy card from your hand to Roserade during your turn, you may use this power. If you attach a [G] Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If you attach a [P] Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Roserade is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Roserade.
Flavor-Text: Its sweet aroma attracts prey. Then it spews poison. The more toxic it is, the sweeter its aroma.

Name: Steelix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Steelix by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.

Name: Torkoal
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Torkoal from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It has an insolent nature that makes it not care about others.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailing finds all food buried deep underground.

Name: Cherrim
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from your Active Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Cherrim is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: During times of strong sunlight, its bud blooms, its petals open fully, and it becomes very active.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

Name: Golbat
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It can drink more than 10 ounces of blood at once. If it has too much, it gets heavy and flies clumsily.

Name: Grotle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A Grotle that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Kakuna and put it onto Kakuna. (This counts as evolving Kakuna.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Although it is a cocoon, it can move a little. It can extend its poison barb if it is attacked.

Name: Metang
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 [P] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When two Beldum fuse together, a magnetic nervous system places their brains in union.

Name: Minun
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

Name: Numel
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: The flaming magma it stores in the hump on its back is the source of its tremendous power.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If tails, Plusle does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The skin on most of its body is moist. However, the skin on its belly spiral feels smooth.

Name: Pupitar
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Pupitar has any Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost of Pupitar is 0.
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Pupitar.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

Name: Pupitar
Flavor-Text: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its fin tips leak poison. Its fins and bones are highly valued as ingredients in herbal medicine.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Tauros is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They fight each other by locking horns. The herd’s protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is a well-established symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

Name: Beldum
Effects: You may switch Beldum with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground’s natural magnetism, letting it float.

Name: Buizel
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It walks around on its tentacles in search of a tree branch where it can dangle down and ambush prey.

Name: Cherubi
Flavor-Text: It evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its big, developed fins move rapidly, allowing it to swim backward while still facing forward.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Larvitar.
Larvitar does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on soil. After it has eaten a large mountain, it will fall asleep so it can grow.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: It is born deep underground. It can’t emerge until it has entirely consumed the soil around it.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: It dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Mankey.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely ill-tempered. Groups of them will attack any handy target for no reason.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

Name: Natu
Effects: Switch Natu with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

Name: Onix
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to Onix, remove a damage counter from Onix.
Effects: Onix can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Onix
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It twists and squirms through the ground. The thunderous roar of its tunneling echoes a long way.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The direction of its belly spiral differs by area. The equator is thought to have an effect on this.

Name: Remoraid
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. Roselia is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

Name: Spearow
Effects: Remove 4 damage counters from Spearow. Spearow can’t retreat during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It flaps its short wings to flush out insects from tall grass. It then plucks them with its stubby beak.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: The shell is soft when it is born. It soon becomes so resilient, prodding fingers will bounce off it.

Name: Stantler
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Teddiursa does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It’s a highly favored tropical snack.

Name: Turtwig
Effects: If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Turtwig.
Flavor-Text: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Vulpix.
Flavor-Text: As it develops, its single white tail gains color and splits into six. It is quite warm and cuddly.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Weedle and put it onto Weedle. (This counts as evolving Weedle.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Capable of flying safely in dark places, it emits ultrasonic cries to check for any obstacles.

Name: Cheerleader’s Cheer
Description: Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Dual Ball
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Emcee’s Chatter
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards. If tails, draw 2 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Energy Returner
Description: Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

Name: Engineer’s Adjustments
Description: Discard an Energy card from your hand. Then, draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Good Rod
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If tails, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

Name: Interviewer’s Questions
Description: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Judge
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Life Herb
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your Pokémon, and remove all Special Conditions and 6 damage counters from that Pokémon (all if there are less than 6).

Name: PlusPower
Description: During this turn, your Pokémon’s attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Pokémon Circulator
Description: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) If you choose a Stage 2 Pokémon in your hand, put that Pokémon on the Basic Pokémon instead of on a Stage 1 Pokémon.

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

Name: Crobat
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Kingdra
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Kingdra is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If your opponent has any [R] Pokémon in play, this attack’s base damage is 20 instead of 60.

Name: Lanturn
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Lanturn’s type is [W] until the end of your turn. This power can’t be used if Lanturn is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

Name: Steelix
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Steelix can’t be affected by any Special Conditions
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Steelix.
You may discard any Stadium card in play.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) (excluding any [D] Pokémon). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

Name: Ursaring
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Ursaring has any damage counters on it, each of Ursaring’s attacks does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent’s deck.

Name: Entei & Raikou LEGEND
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

Discard all Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND. This attack does 80 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers (both yours and your opponent’s). This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

Name: Entei & Raikou LEGEND
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

Discard all Energy attached to Entei & Raikou LEGEND. This attack does 80 damage to each Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers (both yours and your opponent’s). This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Entei & Raikou LEGEND.

Name: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND
Effects: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND does 50 damage to itself and don’t apply Weakness to this damage.
Remove 5 damage counters from Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

Name: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND
Effects: Raikou & Suicune LEGEND does 50 damage to itself and don’t apply Weakness to this damage.
Remove 5 damage counters from Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Raikou & Suicune LEGEND.

Name: Suicune & Entei LEGEND
Effects: Return 2 [W] Energy attached to Suicune & Entei LEGEND to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Suicune & Entei LEGEND.

Name: Suicune & Entei LEGEND
Effects: Return 2 [W] Energy attached to Suicune & Entei LEGEND to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
LEGEND-Rule: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Suicune & Entei LEGEND.
